commands.review_summary:
The branch adds safe record-copy helpers and updates plugin SDK/provider catalog projection paths plus tests so unreadable provider catalog, model, auth, and paired-provider rows are skipped while healthy sibling rows remain.

PR surface: Source +124, Tests +159. Total +283 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. Current main reads provider catalog/model/auth rows without safe per-row guards, and the PR adds synthetic getter/proxy tests that exercise those failure paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide the compatibility/auth-provider semantics; no narrow automated repair finding is present.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- isolates malformed plugin-owned provider catalog/model/auth rows so one bad row does not erase healthy sibling provider output
- adds a small shared safe-record helper for boundary reads that must tolerate unreadable plugin-provided rows
- keeps the slice limited to provider catalog/auth projection, not the broader fuzz branch
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 1 shard, 14 tests passed after rebasing onto current main and resolving the model-catalog type import conflict
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/shared/safe-record.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- touched-file scrub for the reported plugin/tool names, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean after conflict resolution
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_2cc1ad8ec6c5`, lease `cbx_513eb7b47ff1`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: unreadable provider catalog, model, auth, or paired-provider rows are skipped without dropping valid sibling provider catalog output.

Real environment tested: fresh linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree from `origin/main` `8fe50a21362`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: cherry-picked the one-commit provider-catalog slice onto fresh current main, read scoped plugin-sdk/plugins instructions, resolved current-main catalog type import drift, ran focused provider-entry/provider-catalog tests, formatter check, diff check, scrub, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable provider catalog entries, model rows, provider auth option rows, and paired-provider catalog entries are isolated while healthy sibling entries remain visible/registered.

Observed result after fix: provider catalog projection keeps useful healthy output when one plugin-owned row is malformed or unreadable, instead of aborting the whole catalog projection.

What was not tested: no live provider onboarding flow; behavior is covered by the focused SDK/provider catalog tests and the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
